title: Input Attributes
content:
### HTML Input Attributes

---

### Common Input Attributes

| Attribute | Description | Example |
|-----------|-------------|---------|
| `value` | Default value | `value="John"` |
| `placeholder` | Hint text inside input | `placeholder="Enter name"` |
| `readonly` | Cannot modify, but submitted | `readonly` |
| `disabled` | Cannot modify, **not** submitted | `disabled` |
| `required` | Must be filled before submit | `required` |
| `autofocus` | Focus on page load | `autofocus` |
| `maxlength` | Max characters | `maxlength="10"` |
| `minlength` | Min characters | `minlength="3"` |
| `size` | Input field width (chars) | `size="30"` |

---

### Readonly vs Disabled

```html
<!-- User can see but not edit. Value IS submitted -->
<input type="text" value="Read only" readonly>

<!-- User can see but not edit. Value is NOT submitted -->
<input type="text" value="Disabled" disabled>
```

---

### Min, Max, Step

For numeric inputs:

```html
<input type="number" min="0" max="100" step="5">
<input type="date" min="2024-01-01" max="2024-12-31">
<input type="range" min="0" max="10" step="2">
```

---

### Pattern (Regex Validation)

```html
<!-- 3 letters followed by 3 digits -->
<input type="text" pattern="[A-Za-z]{3}[0-9]{3}"
       title="3 letters + 3 digits">
```

---

### Multiple Files

```html
<input type="file" multiple>
```

---

### Autocomplete

```html
<input type="text" autocomplete="on">
<input type="email" autocomplete="email">
```

---

> **Best Practice:** Use `required` for mandatory fields, `placeholder` for hints (not labels), and `pattern` for custom validation.

quiz.question: What is the difference between readonly and disabled?
options: Disabled inputs are submitted, readonly are not
Readonly inputs are submitted, disabled are not
Readonly is for text, disabled for checkboxes
